message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step reasoning:
1) Identify the main node: REG-021 (Art11(3)) empowers the European Commission to adopt delegated acts, per Art97, to amend Annex IV so that technical documentation contains all necessary information to assess system compliance.
2) Retrieve and consider cross-references: Art97(1)-(6) (REG-074 to REG-079) specify conditions, duration, revocation, consultation with Member State experts, notification to Parliament/Council, and objection procedure/time limits for delegated acts.
3) Determine what the clause requires of the organization’s policy: Art11(3) and Art97(1)-(6) set an institutional law-making mechanism for the Commission. They do not impose direct, actionable obligations on a company’s privacy policy content or its internal documentation practices. Any absence of mention of Commission delegated acts, Annex IV, or the Art97 procedure in the company’s policy is an omission, which per instruction 4 must be treated as Non-Deviation.
4) Compare to the provided policy chunk: The text discusses internal evaluation methods, documenting pre-release results, determining evaluation frequency, and providing customer-facing documentation (intended uses, fitness-for-purpose evidence, transparency note for platform services). It neither contradicts nor narrows the Commission’s powers or the Art97 procedure; it simply addresses different operational topics.
5) Conclusion: No explicit contradictions, negations, role misassignments, or timing conflicts with Art11(3) and its Art97 framework are present. Per instruction 4, omissions are Non-Deviation. Therefore, deviation_found = false.
</deliberation>
